differentials.name: DYSPLASIA OF ENDOCHONDRAL OSSIFICATION (PRIMARY SPONGIOSA) =  failure in resorption + remodeling of primary immature spongiosa by osteoclasts
differentials.raw: DYSPLASIA OF ENDOCHONDRAL OSSIFICATION (PRIMARY SPONGIOSA) =  failure in resorption + remodeling of primary immature spongiosa by osteoclasts

differentials.name: DYSPLASIA OF ENDOCHONDRAL OSSIFICATION (SECONDARY SPONGIOSA) =  errors in resorption + remodeling of secondary spongiosa
differentials.raw: DYSPLASIA OF ENDOCHONDRAL OSSIFICATION (SECONDARY SPONGIOSA) =  errors in resorption + remodeling of secondary spongiosa

differentials.name: DYSPLASIA OF INTRAMEMBRANOUS OSSIFICATION =  disequilibrium between periosteal bone formation + endosteal bone resorption
differentials.raw: DYSPLASIA OF INTRAMEMBRANOUS OSSIFICATION =  disequilibrium between periosteal bone formation + endosteal bone resorption

context: Sclerosing Bone Dysplasia Endochondral bone formation: primary spongiosa forms at 7th week of embryogenesis → resorption around 9th week with conversion into secondary spongiosa → osteoclastic remodeling into trabeculae + medullary cavity Target sites for endochondral bone formation: tubular + flat bones, vertebrae, skull base, ethmoids, ends of clavicle Intramembranous ossification: =  transformation of mesenchymal cells into cortical bone without intervening cartilaginous matrix beginning at 9th week of fetal life to beyond closure of growth plates Target sites for intramembranous bone forma
